body_txt: When things look depressing for our comical anime hero, what's the best course of action? Why, go Super-Deformed , sit in a corner, wrap your arms around your knees, and sulk like you've never sulked before! Preferably in a Dramatic Spotlight . Bonus points if you manage to act gloomy enough to attract ghosts or grow mushrooms in your dark, damp corner of woe. The simplest/original form of this trope involves sulking in a literal corner. More derived forms usually include a purple/black background or color filter to emphasize the character's feelings of depression; particularly stylized versions forgo a physical wall or corner and instead have the character/s face away from the camera, crouching or with their heads down, in front of a purple background and/or underneath a vertical hatching pattern to represent their overbearing gloom. A technical term for this might be " latibulating ", an archaic term used around the 17th and 18th centuries to mean "hiding oneself in a corner", although the word itself seems to have fallen out of use well before the animation medium developed. Often combined with Color Failure . Compare Troubled Fetal Position when this position is adopted out of shock rather than sulking. See also Shower of Angst .
